name: Walter Davis
abstract: Walter Davis Jr. (September 2, 1932 – June 2, 1990) was an American bebop and hard bop pianist.

concerts.label: Kool-Newport Jazz Festival: Tribute to Charlie Parker
Kool Jazz Festival: The Blakey Legacy
Kool Jazz Festival: Charlie Haden's Liberation Music Orchestra/Philly Joe Jones' Dameronia
Kool Jazz Festival: Walter Davis Jr.

works.t: Jubilee: Just One of Those Things

concerts.label: Blue Note Records and WBGO Benefit Jazz Concert

about_quotes.speaker: Wilbur Ware
about_quotes.evidence: Blue had Walter Davis, Jr., myself, Al Foster and Lucky had Victor Sproles... We played the job.
about_quotes.doc_title: 68088_Wilbur B. Ware, Sr oral history interview

about_quotes.speaker: Terry Clark
about_quotes.evidence: The interviewee confirms the name 'Walter Davis' but the subsequent narrative describes a 'little kid' playing with a tree branch, whom the interviewee gave drumsticks to. There is no evidence in the text that the 'little kid' is Walter Davis, nor any other interaction between Terry Clark and Walter
about_quotes.doc_title: Terry Clark — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Billy Taylor
about_quotes.evidence: Walter Davis I mean, thank you. Walter Davis and who else? There was another pianist that they recorded as… Harsh Pollen, you know. These were guys who with merchandizing... could have gone much further
about_quotes.doc_title: Billy Taylor — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Jackie McLean
about_quotes.evidence: Walter Davis, a genius, incredibly important in arrangements and things. ... I got Walter Davis. So me and Walter went and got high before we came down to do the show that night.
about_quotes.doc_title: Jackie McLean — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Barry Harris
about_quotes.evidence: Sonny Clarke, Walter Davis, and they came to Detroit. It’s like somebody must have told them, “you better look up that pianist in Detroit” or something.
about_quotes.doc_title: Barry Harris — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Christian McBride
about_quotes.evidence: I had a chance to meet and play with like Red Rodney and Kenny Barron, Curtis Fuller, Jimmy Heath, Walter Davis Jr.
about_quotes.doc_title: Christian McBride interviewed by Monk Rowe, Sarasota, Florida, April 12, 1996
